payload.source_text: The pocket money tracker system allows children to claim pocket money tasks as they are completed throughout the week.
Parents can detail tasks that should be completed and the monetary amount earned for that task.
As a task is completed it is linked to the child that completed it.
The children should be able to see the available tasks and the tasks they have completed that week.
At the end of each week the children can see the total amount they have earned that week.
The Parents can also see a report of how much each child has earned and the tasks they completed so that they can provide the pocket money earned to each child.
At the start of the next week all tasks become available again to be claimed.
Historical task data shall be available.
The resulting system shall be able to operate on iOS 16, Android 9 and Windows 11 or later.

payload.description: Child claiming completed tasks for pocket money
payload.justification: Signal directly expresses the core strategic process of children claiming tasks throughout the week.

payload.description: Parent defining task-based earning structures
payload.justification: Signal establishes the strategic process by which parents set up earning frameworks for children.

payload.description: Providing visibility into task opportunities and completion progress
payload.justification: Signal articulates the strategic process goal of making task data visible to children.

payload.description: Period-end reporting on earnings and payment accountability
payload.justification: Signals describe the strategic reporting process supporting weekly totals and payment accountability.

payload.description: Task as earnable unit with associated monetary value
payload.justification: Signals establish tasks as strategic things that children complete for earnings and parents define with earning structures.

payload.description: Pocket money as accumulated earnings from completed tasks
payload.justification: Signals describe pocket money as the strategic financial entity children earn and view totals for at period end.

payload.description: Child performance record of task completion and earnings
payload.justification: Signals reference completion progress visibility and child performance tracking as strategically important data.

payload.description: Task completion by child yields pocket money earning
payload.justification: Signal establishes the strategic relationship between task completion action and pocket money outcome.

payload.description: Weekly earning and payment cycle
payload.justification: Signals reference tasks throughout the week and weekly totals at period end, establishing a weekly strategic cycle.

payload.description: Period end triggering earning total visibility
payload.justification: Signal describes period end as a strategic event when weekly earning totals become viewable.

payload.description: Child as task completer and pocket money earner
payload.justification: Multiple signals establish children as strategic actors who complete tasks, view progress, and earn pocket money.

payload.description: Parent as task definer and payment administrator
payload.justification: Signals identify parents as strategic actors who define earning structures and require payment accountability.

payload.description: Enable parental oversight of child task performance
payload.justification: Signal articulates the strategic goal of providing parents visibility into how children are performing on tasks.

payload.description: Enable children to earn pocket money through task completion
payload.justification: Signal directly expresses the core strategic goal of the task-based earning system.

payload.description: Empower parents to structure and control child earning opportunities
payload.justification: Signal articulates the strategic goal of giving parents control over task-earning definitions.

payload.description: Provide children transparency into available opportunities and personal progress
payload.justification: Signal defines the strategic goal of giving children visibility into tasks and their own completion status.

payload.description: Support payment accountability through earning visibility
payload.justification: Signals establish the strategic goal of ensuring transparency in what has been earned and should be paid.

payload.description: Child claiming pocket money tasks upon completion
payload.justification: Signal explicitly describes the core business process of children claiming completed tasks.

payload.description: Parent defining tasks with associated monetary values
payload.justification: Signal establishes parent capability to define tasks as a business process.

payload.description: Weekly aggregation of child earnings at period end
payload.justification: Signal describes the aggregation capability as a recurring business process.

payload.description: Parent payment reconciliation linking children to earned amounts and completed tasks
payload.justification: Signal describes parent reporting capability as a payment reconciliation process.

payload.description: Visibility of available tasks to child users
payload.justification: Signal specifies child visibility over available tasks as a business function.

payload.description: Visibility of personally completed tasks within current week to child users
payload.justification: Signal specifies child visibility over their own completed tasks as a business function.

payload.description: Retention and accessibility of historical task completion data
payload.justification: Signal mandates historical data retention as an ongoing business function.

payload.description: All tasks restore to available status at weekly cycle boundary
payload.justification: Signal explicitly defines this as a business rule governing task status transitions.

payload.description: Child as a business entity participating in the pocket money system
payload.justification: Multiple signals explicitly reference 'child' as a core business entity that claims tasks, completes tasks, earns money, and is reported upon.

payload.description: Task as a business entity with monetary value and completion status
payload.justification: Task is central to multiple signals: defined by parents with monetary values, tracked by completion status, visible in different states, reset weekly, and retained historically.

payload.description: Parent as a business entity with task definition and payment reconciliation responsibilities
payload.justification: Parent is explicitly referenced as the entity that defines tasks and uses reporting for payment reconciliation.

payload.description: Monetary value associated with each task
payload.justification: Tasks are explicitly described as having associated monetary values that are defined by parents.

payload.description: Task completion status distinguishing available from completed states
payload.justification: Signals explicitly reference task state categories (available vs. completed) and status transitions during weekly reset.

payload.description: Total earnings amount aggregated per child for the period
payload.justification: Weekly aggregation capability and payment reconciliation both reference child earnings as a calculated attribute.

payload.description: Association between child and completed tasks performed by that child
payload.justification: Multiple signals establish the conceptual relationship linking children to the tasks they have completed.

payload.description: Association between parent and tasks they have defined
payload.justification: Parent capability to define tasks implies a business relationship between parent entity and task entity.

payload.description: Weekly business cycle governing task claiming and earnings aggregation
payload.justification: Multiple signals reference the weekly cycle as the temporal framework for task completion, aggregation, and reset.

payload.description: Task completion event triggering claim by child
payload.justification: Signals describe task completion as a business event that triggers claiming behavior.

payload.description: Weekly period end event triggering earnings aggregation
payload.justification: Signal describes period end as the timing event for aggregation capability.

payload.description: Weekly cycle boundary triggering task status reset to available
payload.justification: Signal explicitly defines cycle boundary as the trigger for task reset business rule.

payload.description: Child role claiming and completing pocket money tasks
payload.justification: Signals describe children as business actors with specific task-related responsibilities.

payload.description: Parent role defining tasks and reconciling payments
payload.justification: Signals describe parents as business actors with task definition and payment responsibilities.

payload.description: Enable children to earn pocket money through task completion and facilitate parent payment reconciliation
payload.justification: Core business concept implies the underlying goal of the pocket money system. | Payment reconciliation capability implies a business goal of supporting parent payment processes.

payload.description: Child visibility restricted to available tasks and own completed tasks within current week
payload.justification: Signal defines visibility requirements as business constraints on what children can see.

payload.description: Historical task completion data must be retained beyond current period
payload.justification: Signal mandates retention as a business constraint on data lifecycle.

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL CellContentItem elements present in the ledger.

payload.name: Family Financial Stewardship
payload.description: Enterprise accountability for establishing and administering the economic relationship between parents and children through structured earning mechanisms, defining payment obligations, and maintaining performance records.

payload.name: Child Economic Participation
payload.description: Enterprise commitment to enabling children as participants in the family economic framework through transparent access to earning opportunities, completion mechanisms, and visibility into personal progress and accumulated value.

payload.name: Task Stewardship
payload.description: Business responsibility for defining, publishing, and governing the lifecycle of remunerative tasks including their monetary valuation, availability status, and periodic restoration to enable ongoing participation in the pocket money system.

payload.name: Participation Entitlement
payload.description: Business responsibility for governing child participation rights in claiming and completing tasks, establishing visibility boundaries for task discovery and personal performance within active business cycles.

payload.name: Earnings Settlement
payload.description: Business responsibility for aggregating child earnings at period boundaries, reconciling payments against completed work, and enabling parental fulfillment of remuneration obligations derived from task completion.

payload.name: Performance Record
payload.description: Business responsibility for retaining historical evidence of task completion beyond active business cycles to support accountability, auditability, and longitudinal visibility of participation patterns.

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Domain elements present in the ledger.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall maintain pocket money as accumulated earnings from completed tasks.
payload.rationale: Establishes pocket money as an entity representing the cumulative result of task completion, with its derivation from completed tasks as a core attribute.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall maintain child performance records of task completion and earnings.
payload.rationale: Defines the enterprise obligation to maintain records tracking both completion events and financial outcomes for each child.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall establish that task completion by a child yields pocket money earning.
payload.rationale: Defines the causal relationship between task completion events and pocket money accumulation as a structural rule of the enterprise domain.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall enable parents to define task-based earning structures.
payload.rationale: Functional capability derived from the process of parent-defined earning structures, the parent actor role as definer, and the goal of empowering parental control over earning opportunities.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall provide period-end reporting on earnings.
payload.rationale: Reporting process capability supporting earning visibility.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall be accountable for payment.
payload.rationale: Enterprise accountability commitment governing payment obligations.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall operate on a weekly earning cycle.
payload.rationale: Temporal constraint establishing the recurring cycle governing earning accumulation.
payload.fit_criteria: Earnings are totaled at seven-day intervals.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall operate on a weekly payment cycle.
payload.rationale: Temporal constraint establishing the recurring cycle governing payment obligations.
payload.fit_criteria: Payments are administered at seven-day intervals.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall ensure period end triggers earning total visibility.
payload.rationale: Event-driven functional capability where period-end event causes earning totals to become visible, supporting payment accountability.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall enable parental oversight of child task performance.
payload.rationale: Governance constraint expressing the enterprise commitment to provide parents with visibility and control over child performance, distinct from the specific mechanisms.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall recognise pocket money as accumulated earnings from completed tasks.
payload.rationale: Entity CCI establishes pocket money as accumulated earnings, defining its composition and derivation.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall enable children to claim completed tasks for pocket money.
payload.rationale: Process CCI, Actor CCI, and Goal CCI converge on the enterprise commitment to enable the claiming capability for children.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall provide children visibility into available tasks.
payload.rationale: Process CCI expresses the enterprise obligation to provide transparency into task opportunities.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall provide children visibility into task completion progress.
payload.rationale: Goal CCI expresses the enterprise obligation to provide transparency into progress.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall establish a weekly earning cycle.
payload.rationale: Temporal constraint governing the periodicity of earning operations.
payload.fit_criteria: Earning cycle repeats on a seven-day interval.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall establish a weekly payment cycle.
payload.rationale: Temporal constraint governing the periodicity of payment operations.
payload.fit_criteria: Payment cycle repeats on a seven-day interval.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain pocket money as an aggregated monetary amount derived from completed tasks.
payload.rationale: Row 2 structural obligation defining pocket money as an accumulated earnings amount entity maintained by the business.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain total earnings as a monetary amount aggregated per child for each period.

payload.statement: The system shall make child earnings totals visible to parents at period end.
payload.rationale: Row 2 system affordance complementing the parent reporting capability, naming the visibility provided by the system.

payload.statement: The parent shall be accountable for reconciling earned amounts to children.
payload.rationale: Row 2 named business role accountability constraint, identifying the internal party responsible for payment reconciliation outside the system boundary.

payload.statement: The parent shall be accountable for fulfilling payment of earned amounts to children.
payload.rationale: Row 2 named business role accountability constraint, identifying the internal party responsible for payment fulfillment outside the system boundary.

payload.statement: The business shall operate task claiming on a weekly cycle.
payload.rationale: Row 2 business cycle constraint governing the temporal rhythm of task claiming operations.

payload.statement: The business shall operate earnings aggregation on a weekly cycle.
payload.rationale: Row 2 business cycle constraint governing the temporal rhythm of earnings aggregation operations.

payload.statement: The business shall operate payment reconciliation on a weekly cycle.
payload.rationale: Row 2 business cycle constraint governing the temporal rhythm of payment settlement.

payload.statement: The business shall aggregate child earnings at the weekly period end event.
payload.rationale: Row 2 business process obligation triggered at period boundary to compute accumulated earnings.

payload.statement: The system shall make aggregated earnings totals visible at period end.
payload.rationale: Row 2 system affordance making earnings totals available at the boundary following aggregation.

payload.statement: The system shall make child task completion associations visible to parents.
payload.rationale: Row 2 system affordance complementing parental oversight capability.

payload.statement: The business shall recognise pocket money as a monetary amount accumulated from task completion.
payload.rationale: Row 2 structural obligation defining the business's conceptual recognition of pocket money as an earnings entity.

payload.statement: The system shall make tasks claimable by children upon completion.
payload.rationale: Row 2 system affordance complementing the child claiming capability, naming the black-box claimability provided by the system.

payload.statement: The system shall make available tasks visible to children.
payload.rationale: Row 2 system affordance complementing child task discovery capability.

payload.statement: The system shall make a child's personally completed tasks within the current week visible to that child.
payload.rationale: Row 2 system affordance complementing child progress visibility capability.

payload.statement: The business shall restrict child visibility to available tasks within the current week.
payload.rationale: Row 2 business rule constraining the scope of child visibility to available tasks for privacy and simplicity.

payload.statement: The business shall restrict child visibility to own completed tasks within the current week.
payload.rationale: Row 2 business rule constraining the scope of child visibility to own completed tasks for privacy and simplicity.

payload.statement: The business shall enforce a weekly earning cycle governing task claiming.
payload.rationale: Row 2 business cycle constraint establishing the temporal governance rule for task claiming.

payload.statement: The business shall enforce a weekly earning cycle governing earnings aggregation.
payload.rationale: Row 2 business cycle constraint establishing the temporal governance rule for earnings aggregation.

payload.statement: The business shall restore all tasks to available status at the weekly cycle boundary.
payload.rationale: Row 2 business process obligation to reset task availability at cycle boundaries, enabling ongoing participation.

payload.statement: The business shall enforce a weekly cycle governing task claiming.
payload.rationale: Weekly cycle boundary constraint governing task claiming process derived from When-column CCI.
payload.fit_criteria: Weekly cycle boundary is consistently applied to task claiming process

payload.statement: The business shall enforce a weekly cycle governing earnings aggregation.
payload.rationale: Weekly cycle boundary constraint governing earnings aggregation process derived from When-column CCI.
payload.fit_criteria: Weekly cycle boundary is consistently applied to earnings aggregation process

payload.statement: The business shall aggregate child earnings at weekly period end.
payload.rationale: Weekly payment cycle necessitates periodic settlement of earnings; this Row 2 business responsibility triggers at the cycle boundary to calculate payments owed.
payload.fit_criteria: Child earnings are aggregated once per weekly cycle at period end

payload.statement: The business shall maintain a record of each task completion performed by a child.
payload.rationale: CCI-ROW2-C-How-007 (How/Function) expresses the business responsibility for retaining and making accessible historical task completion data

payload.statement: The business shall retain historical task completion data beyond the current business period.
payload.rationale: CCI-ROW2-C-Why-003 (Why/Constraint) imposes a retention rule requiring data to persist beyond active cycles

payload.statement: A child can claim a pocket money task.
payload.rationale: Who-column actor boundary-crossing statement. The CCI describes an external party (child role) interacting with the system to claim tasks. Per boundary test: the child acts THROUGH the system (reaches in) → ACTOR subject. Uses the actor's real action verb 'claim' rather than burying the actor in system-enable phrasing. This is a first-class boundary statement naming the system boundary at the business-owner level.

payload.statement: A child can complete a pocket money task.
payload.rationale: Who-column actor boundary-crossing statement. The CCI explicitly describes both claiming AND completing tasks as child role actions. These are distinct obligations (claim vs. complete are different actions with different business semantics) — atomicity requires separate requirements. The child completing a task is a boundary-crossing interaction (actor subject), using the real action verb 'complete'.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain tasks as entities with associated monetary value and completion status.
payload.rationale: Establishes the structural foundation of the task entity required to realize the enterprise's obligation to link task completion to pocket money earning.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain the association between each child and the tasks completed by that child.
payload.rationale: Establishes the structural relationship required to trace which child completed which task, enabling the earning attribution at the heart of the seed obligation.

payload.statement: The business shall aggregate child earnings from the monetary values of completed tasks.
payload.rationale: Realizes the seed's obligation by defining the business process that transforms task completion into pocket money earning through monetary aggregation.

payload.statement: A child can claim a task upon its completion.
payload.rationale: Captures the boundary-crossing actor capability that initiates the completion-to-earning chain; the child's claiming action is the mechanism by which completion yields earning.

payload.statement: The system shall make available tasks claimable by child users.
payload.rationale: Defines the system affordance that enables the claiming capability; complements the actor-subject requirement by specifying what the system provides at the boundary.

payload.name: task monetary value
payload.description: The business shall maintain tasks as entities with associated monetary value and completion status.

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL DataDictionaryEntry elements present in the ledger.

payload.title: High Level Description
payload.description: Section: High Level Description

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Segment elements present in the ledger.

payload.description: Expresses the core purpose of enabling children to claim completed tasks for pocket money throughout the week.
Establishes parental motivation to define task-based earning structures for children.
Articulates the goal of providing children visibility into task opportunities and their own completion progress.
Defines the objective of enabling children to view weekly earning totals at period end.
Establishes parental need for reporting to support payment accountability and child performance visibility.
Declares the presence of a high-level conceptual overview section for the system description.
Defines the core business concept of children claiming pocket money tasks upon completion within a weekly cycle.
Establishes the parent capability to define tasks as business entities with associated monetary values.
Specifies the conceptual relationship between completed tasks and the child entity that performed them.
Defines child visibility requirements over two task state categories: available and personally completed within the current week.
Establishes the weekly aggregation capability showing total earnings per child at period end.
Defines parent reporting capability linking children to their earned amounts and completed tasks for payment reconciliation.
Specifies the weekly reset business rule that restores all tasks to available status at cycle boundary.
Mandates retention and accessibility of historical task completion data beyond the current period.

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Signal elements present in the ledger.
This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Stakeholder elements present in the ledger.

payload.description: Ambiguous whether this expresses a strategic goal for auditability or merely a functional requirement for data retention.

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Concern elements present in the ledger.
